sources.label: Islington Council parade page
sources.note: Official source for date, 14:00 start time, and route updates.

sources.label: Islington possible route and closures map
sources.note: Council PDF showing the possible route/closure corridor. Check again before travelling.

sources.label: TfL travel updates
sources.note: Use on Wi-Fi before travelling for final station and service changes.

route.label: Provisional parade corridor
route.note: Council possible-route corridor: stadium area, Drayton Park, Aubert Park, Highbury Grove, St Pauls Road, Upper Street, Town Hall area. Confirm before travel.

pois.name: Stadium area
pois.note: Expected start area. Use official stewarding and road-closure guidance on the day.

pois.name: Drayton Park
pois.note: Nearby rail station; check event access before relying on it.

pois.name: Highbury Fields
pois.note: Open-space fallback landmark if the crowd gets tight.

pois.name: Highbury & Islington
pois.note: Very busy. Have a walking fallback.

pois.name: Islington Town Hall area
pois.note: Likely civic endpoint area. Confirm before travel.

pois.name: Angel
pois.note: Useful southern exit if Upper Street is crowded.

pois.name: Ask steward for nearest first aid
pois.note: Placeholder until official first-aid points are published.

pois.name: Stewarded route area
pois.note: Follow Met Police, steward and council directions over this offline pack.

pois.name: Highbury & Islington · south exit
pois.note: Toward Upper Street. Expect long queues at peak.

pois.name: Highbury & Islington · Fields exit
pois.note: Quieter side, opens onto Highbury Fields.

pois.name: Holloway Road · station
pois.note: Walking fallback north of the stadium area.

pois.name: Caledonian Road · station
pois.note: West-side escape if Holloway is shut.

pois.name: Angel · south exit
pois.note: Southern escape via Upper Street.

pois.name: Highbury Fields · north toilet block
pois.note: Council-maintained. Expect queues.

pois.name: Highbury Fields · south block
pois.note: Quieter than the north block.

pois.name: Town Hall public WC
pois.note: Check signage on the day.

pois.name: Islington Green · WC
pois.note: Verify open hours before relying on it.

pois.name: Angel Square area · WC
pois.note: Near the Angel southern exit.

pois.name: Event portaloos
pois.note: Placeholder; follow steward signs on the day.

pois.name: Highbury Fields · drinking fountain
pois.note: Bring a bottle to refill.

pois.name: Town Hall · refill point
pois.note: Public refill station.

pois.name: Highbury Corner area · refill
pois.note: Several cafés will refill for free if asked.

pois.name: Angel area · shops sell water
pois.note: Last reliable refill before heading south.

pois.name: ATMs · Upper St (north)
pois.note: Several bank branches near Highbury Corner.

pois.name: ATMs · Upper St (mid)
pois.note: Inside grocery and convenience stores.

pois.name: ATMs · Angel
pois.note: Several free-to-use machines near the station.

pois.name: ATM · Town Hall area
pois.note: Single machine; carry a backup card.

pois.name: Highbury Fields · south lawn
pois.note: Quieter pocket if the crowd gets tight.

pois.name: Liverpool Road · quieter pavement
pois.note: Parallel to Upper Street, much less dense.

pois.name: Canonbury Square
pois.note: Calm green square away from the main flow.

pois.name: Barnsbury side streets
pois.note: Residential, low foot traffic.

pois.name: Highbury Corner · open view
pois.note: Wide pavement; good sight line of the bus turn.

pois.name: Upper Street · mid kerb
pois.note: Less crowded view if you arrive early.

pois.name: Town Hall · steps area
pois.note: Elevated vantage at the civic endpoint.

closures.label: Road closures
closures.note: Closures are expected around the route. This pack will be updated as official details firm up.

closures.label: Tube and rail access
closures.note: Station entry may change at short notice for crowd control. Keep a walking fallback.

stations.name: Arsenal
stations.note: Closest to the start area; likely very busy.

stations.name: Holloway Road
stations.note: Check before travelling; event controls may change access.

stations.name: Drayton Park
stations.note: National Rail station near the stadium area.

stations.name: Angel
stations.note: Useful southern exit if Upper Street is busy.

stepFreeRoutesOut.label: Southbound fallback
stepFreeRoutesOut.note: Use if northern stations are blocked or crowded.

stepFreeRoutesOut.label: North/east fallback
stepFreeRoutesOut.via: Highbury Fields, then quieter side streets
stepFreeRoutesOut.note: Avoid forcing through dense crowds to reach a closed station.

safety.heading: If the crowd stops moving
safety.body: Stay upright, keep arms in front of your chest, and move diagonally with the flow rather than pushing against it.

safety.heading: If you lose your group
safety.body: Do not fight back through the densest area. Go to your fallback point at the agreed time.

safety.heading: If signal dies
safety.body: Airplane mode is fine, but keep Location Services on. Your GPS dot can still work without internet.

safety.heading: If you need help
safety.body: Show the My location card to a steward. It has your coordinates, accuracy radius, and nearest landmark.

scheduleEstimate.label: Parade starts at the stadium area
Crowds disperse toward stations and side streets

chants.title: North London Forever
chants.cue: North London forever / whatever the weather
chants.detail:
Start: "North London forever".
Slow anthem, scarves up, let the chorus breathe.
Best moment: bus in sight, crowd already singing.

chants.title: One-nil to The Arsenal
chants.detail:
Start: "One-nil".
Short, old-school, repeatable.
Use after someone mentions clean sheets, Raya, Gabriel, or Saliba.

chants.title: 49 undefeated
chants.detail:
Start: "49".
Keep it punchy: call, clap, repeat.
A history flex for the queue, the pub, or any Spurs-adjacent banter.

chants.title: Arsenal till I die
chants.detail:
Start: "Arsenal".
Simple loyalty chant.
Good when the crowd needs one everyone can join without checking words.

chants.title: Greatest team
chants.cue: We're by far the greatest team
chants.detail:
Start: "by far".
Big grin energy, no subtlety.
Repeat twice, clap on the gaps, keep it clean around families.

chants.title: Oh to be a Gooner
chants.detail:
Start: "Oh to be".
Fast chant, easy lift.
Works while walking between stations or waiting for the next bus glimpse.

chants.title: Allez Allez Allez
chants.detail:
Start: "Allez".
European-night rhythm.
Use as a rolling chant when the street is moving and people can clap along.

chants.title: Set-piece again
chants.detail:
Start: "Set-piece".
For the corner-kick era and Gabriel chaos.
Short enough to use as a joke every time someone points at a lamp post.

chants.title: We love you Arsenal
chants.cue: We love you Arsenal, we do
chants.detail:
Start: "We love you".
Warm, family-safe, simple.
Best when the bus slows or players wave directly at your section.

chants.title: North Bank / Clock End
chants.detail:
Start: "North Bank".
Old-ground call-and-response.
Let one side answer the other if the street splits naturally.

chants.title: Come on you reds
chants.detail:
Start: "Come on".
The emergency chant: no setup, no memory test.
Use when something needs to start right now.

chants.title: Up the Arsenal
chants.detail:
Start: "Up".
Tiny phrase, massive usefulness.
A quick shout after a tap, photo, wave, or group reunion.

chants.title: Saliba
chants.cue: Da da da da da, Saliba
chants.detail:
Start: rhythm first, then "Saliba".
Defender chant, arms up, bouncing optional.
Keep the beat more than the words.

chants.title: Rice Rice Baby
chants.detail:
Start: "Rice".
Midfield engine chant.
Best after trivia, player vote, or any mention of running forever.

chants.title: Bukayo Saka
chants.detail:
Start: "Bukayo".
Keep it bright and clean.
Short player chant for families, kids, and shirts with number 7 everywhere.

chants.title: Odegaard Oi Oi
chants.detail:
Start: "Martin".
Captain chant, clipped rhythm.
Good for a quick lift without turning the whole street into a mosh pit.

chants.title: Super Mik Arteta
chants.detail:
Start: "Super Mik".
Manager appreciation, loud but tidy.
Use after season-moment votes or when someone starts talking tactics.

chants.title: Kai Havertz scores again
chants.detail:
Start: "Kai".
Forward chant with a wink.
Good when the crowd is playful and someone wants a proper terrace bit.

chants.title: His name is Gabi
chants.detail:
Start: "Gabi".
Use for Gabriel, Martinelli, or general Gabi confusion.
If in doubt: point at the shirt and laugh.

chants.title: Trossard again
chants.detail:
Start: "Trossard".
Super-sub energy, quick repeat.
Save it for the person who brought snacks out of nowhere.

trivia.question: Who felt like the season workload monster?
trivia.explainer: Rice was the obvious all-action answer in midfield: starts, minutes, set pieces, duels, everything.

trivia.question: Who is the safest pass-accuracy shout?
trivia.explainer: Saliba is the calm centre-back answer: short exits, switches, and pressure-proof recycling.

trivia.question: Who anchors the clean-sheet story?
trivia.explainer: Raya is the keeper answer, with Gabriel and Saliba doing the heavy defensive lifting in front.

trivia.question: Who is the set-piece menace?
trivia.explainer: Gabriel is the obvious box-crashing pick: corners, blocks, headers, panic.

trivia.question: Who is the captain-creator hub?
trivia.explainer: Odegaard is the organiser answer: press trigger, right-half-space passing, captain energy.

trivia.question: Which young player gets the academy spark card?
trivia.explainer: Nwaneri is the local excitement answer: young, brave, and instantly chantable.
